reasoning: Lilly King holds the women's long course 100m breaststroke world record of 1:04.13, set in 2017. As of October 2025, there are no pending faster records listed on Wikipedia. The period from October to January is heavily dominated by short-course (25m) and short-course yards competitions, such as the World Aquatics Swimming World Cup in Oct/Nov 2025. Given the lack of major long-course international events scheduled during this winter window, and the fact that the record has stood for over 8 years, it is highly unlikely that anyone will break the long-course world record by January 24, 2026. Probabilities decrease slightly for later dates to account for the increasing horizon.
